data.title: As a developer, ui-discover route crawling uses @playwright/cli instead of Python sync_api
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Migrate ui-discover's route crawling from Python Playwright sync_api to @playwright/cli Bash commands. Replace page navigation, DOM inspection, and route crawling logic that currently uses sync_playwright with discrete CLI commands (goto, snapshot, eval). Update the static-analysis-only fallback to check for CLI availability instead of Python Playwright.
**Why**: ui-discover uses a fundamentally different Playwright interface (Python sync_api) than the other 4 skills (MCP). The CLI migration unifies all browser automation on a single interface.
**Scope**:
- IN: Replace Python sync_api usage with CLI commands; update availability check from Python import to CLI binary check; update cache-format-reference.md Playwright metadata fields; verify CLI output schema compatibility with existing cache consumers
- OUT: Changes to the UI discovery cache format (structure preserved); changes to static-analysis-only fallback logic (behavior preserved)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, ui-discover route crawling uses @playwright/cli Bash commands with no references to Python sync_api or sync_playwright imports
  ← Satisfies: "All 5 skills use @playwright/cli commands via Bash instead of MCP tools"
- When this story is complete, the UI discovery cache produced by CLI-based crawling is compatible with existing cache consumers (same field names in manifest.json)
  ← Satisfies: "All 5 skills use @playwright/cli commands via Bash"
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Reliability] Python sync_api returns structured objects; CLI returns JSON strings — verify field naming compatibility
- [Reliability] Multi-page route crawling requires scripting multiple CLI invocations or using run-code with loop logic
- [Testing] Existing tests that mock Python Playwright will need updating to mock CLI output
